intake.client_position: Stripe annual report shows $24K gross; $19.5K net of refunds and chargebacks. Schedule C Line 1 reported $19.5K.

analysis: CP2000 is an automated underreporter notice; 30-day response deadline (May 1, 2025; 16 days remaining as of intake date). NOT a statutory notice of deficiency; 90-day Tax Court window is preserved if CP3219A or Letter 3219 issued later. Form 1099-K under §6050W reports GROSS payments through third-party network; refunds and chargebacks are NOT excluded at the 1099-K level. Schedule C may report gross receipts on Line 1 with refunds and chargebacks deducted as an expense (typically Line 22 'other deductions') or net on Line 1 with explanation. The taxpayer reported $19.5K net; the IRS sees $24K gross from 1099-K and assumes underreporting.
conclusion: Strategy: disagree with substantiation. Draft response to CP2000 attaching: (1) Stripe annual statement showing $24K gross + refunds and chargebacks bringing net to $19.5K; (2) Schedule C Line 1 reconciliation with explanation of net-reporting methodology; (3) reference to §6050W gross reporting nature. Recommend response by certified mail with return receipt by May 1, 2025. If IRS proceeds with adjustment after response, expect CP3219A statutory notice (90-day Tax Court rights). Future-year recommendation: report gross on Schedule C Line 1 + refunds/chargebacks on Line 22 to align with 1099-K reporting.
band_rationale: Substantiation supports the client's position; reconciliation between gross and net is a routine CP2000 dispute.

authorities.quoted_text: Gross income includes all income from whatever source derived.

authorities.citation: Form 1040 Schedule C (2023) Instructions, Line 1 Gross Receipts
